component.name: CloudSync
component.summary: Two-way cloud sync for Linux — Google Drive, Dropbox, S3, and more

description.p: CloudSync is a native Linux desktop app that keeps your local folders in perfect sync with your cloud storage — automatically, in the background. Save a file and it's in the cloud within seconds. Changes from other devices download on your chosen schedule.

ul.li: Two-way sync with Google Drive, Dropbox, Microsoft OneDrive, Amazon S3, Backblaze B2, and Cloudflare R2
Real-time local file watching — uploads within ~2 seconds of a save
Per-folder sync intervals and conflict resolution strategies
Smart conflict resolution: keep both copies, local wins, or remote wins
Runs quietly in the system tray with desktop notifications
Step-by-step setup wizard — no command line required
Your credentials stay on your machine and are never sent to SeraVault

description.p: CloudSync is free to use with one cloud provider and one sync folder. A subscription ($9.99/year) removes all limits on providers and sync folders.

screenshot.caption: CloudSync main window showing sync folders and status

ul.li: Two-way sync with Google Drive, Dropbox, Microsoft OneDrive, Amazon S3, Backblaze B2, and Cloudflare R2
Real-time local file watching with ~2 second upload latency
Per-folder sync intervals and conflict resolution strategies
In-app sign-in via embedded browser with external browser fallback
System tray icon and desktop notifications
Free tier: 1 provider, 1 sync folder. Subscription unlocks everything.
